kept.text:
Sverige har fått fram Spotify, Klarna, King, Minecraft, Lovable osv.

Hur kan vi inte ha löst bättre mjukvara för vården? Ena dagen är det Millennium, nästa är det Cosmic.

profile_bio.description:
Nordnets Investeringscoach. Utsedd till
Årets Sparprofil 2024

kept.text:
Stockholm will be the tech powerhouse of Europe.

You can walk into a real startup event any day of the week now. Not the old invite only VC dinners. Actual community stuff. Hackathons, operators sharing playbooks, fireside chats, product and design workshops. 

Companies opening their doors and giving away knowledge that used to stay locked inside teams. Most of it free and fully open.

Teenagers building their first company sit next to veterans starting their next one. The full mix is showing up.

Sweden has always been good at riding technological shifts even when we don't build the underlying platforms. 

This feels like one of those moments. 

A new wave of hackers out building and spotting opportunities everywhere.

The investing side is widening too. First time angels writing checks, not just the usual insiders.

Global funds fly in every week. US firms move fast, local funds compete, and I've seen pre seed and seed rounds of 3 to 15 million close in days. 

The government is even looking at allowing private assets in ISK, which would open up private company investing to far more people.

And all of it sits within a short walk. 

The internet and mobile generation with Spotify, King, Klarna. 

The digitize the physical world generation with Voi, Kry, Einride, Instabee. 

Now the AI first wave with Sana, Tandem and Lovable.

A new era is starting in Stockholm.

kept.text:
Stop dropshipping in the USA as a beginner.

Start in NL/BE instead:

- iDEAL/Bancontact = no chargebacks
- Easiest market for beginners

Then expand to:

- Germany → Strong EU market, stable results
- Sweden → Easy high ROAS (watch Klarna carefully)

Other easy markets: Norway, UK, Australia, Japan, Finland, Italy

Move to USA only after €1M revenue.

kept.text:
Caleb Hammer breaks down how a 5 dollar energy drink becomes a debt spiral

"People are buying $5 energy drinks every day on the way to work and that shit stacks, then they put it on a credit card building interest, or even worse at 35% on Klarna"

"They buy energy drinks on Klarna, pay Klarna with their credit card, pay the credit card with a personal loan… it's just debt for debt for debt"

profile_bio.description: clipper | @counterpartytv | biz: @eyekonstudio

kept.text:
After being a Klarna $KLAR hater since the IPO (down 65% since) am now willing to revisit my stance: 

1) At $5B $KLAR is trading close to net cash 
2) We are now past the vesting cliff and appear to have bottomed-out
3) I am encouraged by their ability to forward sell and securitize receivables off balance sheet, positioning themselves primarily as an originator (similar to the much hated $CVNA)
4) EU banking license 

Will be digging deeper.

kept.text:
We are thrilled to welcome @privy_io as a Supporting Partner for HYPE Miami!🌴

Privy provides secure, scalable onboarding and wallet infrastructure for leading applications built on crypto rails. 2000+ developers and businesses, including Klarna and Hyperliquid, use Privy to power over 120 million accounts and process billions of dollars in volume every month. Privy was acquired by Stripe in 2025 and continues to operate independently, helping developers build the next generation of onchain products.

Catch the team at Queen Miami Beach in less than a week!
